questions.question: Consider an integrated moving average (IMA) process of order (p, d, q). Derive the general form of the IMA model and explain the role of the integrated term (d) in the model. ( marks: 10)
Suppose we have an IMA(3, 1, 2) model. If the series is differenced once to make it stationary, what will be the resulting IMA model? Provide a detailed explanation. ( marks: 8)

A time series is modeled as an IMA(2, 1, 1). The series is known to have a unit root. How would you forecast this time series using the IMA model? Explain your approach, including the use of the Augmented Dickey-Fuller test or other suitable methods. ( marks: 12)
